skill.name: mixpanel-analytics
skill.description: Guides implementation and review of MixPanel analytics events in a Django optimo_analytics module, with checks for PII, schema design, service patterns, and tests.
skill.summary: Implement and review Django MixPanel analytics events with PII and schema safeguards.
skill.author: DiversioTeam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and blocker patterns, but review shows most high-severity crypto and secret findings are false positives from documentation text and MixPanel terminology. The confirmed risk is that the skill directs agents to run shell commands and edit repository files during analytics implementation and review, which is legitimate for the task but requires user trust and scoped execution.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed Shell Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run repository shell commands for git state, grep scans, xargs processing, loops, sed, and test commands. These commands are task-relevant, but they execute in the user workspace and could read broad repository content or behave differently in unusual project layouts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited sections contain explicit bash commands and command substitution loops. The intent is legitimate repository analysis, but the external command execution capability is real.

medium_findings.title: Repository File Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: Implementation mode asks the agent to create or modify constants, schemas, registry entries, service helpers, exports, business logic, and tests. This is expected for a coding skill, but it gives the skill a meaningful ability to change application behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The implementation checklist explicitly instructs file edits across multiple analytics modules. This is a legitimate capability but increases operational risk if used outside the intended codebase.

low_findings.title: False Positive Secret and Environment Access Finding
low_findings.description: The static env_access finding points to text that names an API key identifier fallback, not code that reads environment variables or extracts secrets. No evidence found of environment secret access in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe distinct_id prefixes such as apikey_<id>. They do not reference environment variables, credential files, or secret exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography and Device File Findings
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts are caused by documentation terms such as MixPanel and event examples, not cryptographic APIs. The filesystem device alerts are shell stderr redirections to /dev/null in example commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited areas shows Markdown documentation and ordinary shell redirection. No hashing, encryption, or device-file data access behavior is present.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: No evidence found of instructions that impersonate system messages, request security review bypass, claim pre-approval, or tell the evaluator to ignore prior instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: A targeted review of the skill text did not find prompt-injection phrases. Confidence is limited because natural-language variants can be subtle.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Pipelines and Command Substitution in Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash snippets with grep, xargs, command substitution, while loops, sed, and project-specific test commands. These are useful validation steps but should run only in trusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command patterns are directly visible in bash code blocks. They are not malicious by themselves, but they are executable workflow instructions.

content.value_statement: Analytics changes can leak PII or break event schemas when teams add tracking quickly. This skill guides Django MixPanel implementation and review with checklists for privacy, registry coverage, and tests.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of MixPanel event constants, schemas, registry entries, service helpers, exports, and tests.
Reviews analytics implementations for PII fields, UUID typing, event registration, and schema design.
Checks service method patterns such as keyword-only arguments, fire-and-forget behavior, and structured logging.
Provides validation commands for linting, type checks, Django checks, and analytics test execution.
Defines event naming, distinct_id fallback, cron timestamp, and MixPanel timestamp conventions.
limitations: It is tailored to the Django4Lyfe optimo_analytics module and may not fit other MixPanel architectures.
It relies on repository shell commands and file edits, so users should run it only in trusted workspaces.
It does not connect to MixPanel or validate live production event delivery.
It cannot guarantee privacy compliance without human review of business context and data policy.

use_cases.title: Add a New Product Event
use_cases.description: Implement a new analytics event with constants, schema validation, service tracking, and focused tests.

use_cases.title: Audit Analytics Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Review staged or branch changes for PII exposure, missing registry entries, schema mistakes, and weak service patterns.

use_cases.title: Standardize Event Design
use_cases.description: Apply consistent naming, distinct_id selection, timestamp handling, and cron tracking rules across a Django analytics module.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a Basic Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mixpanel-analytics skill to implement tracking for a new successful user action in optimo_analytics. Follow the seven-step checklist and add tests.

prompt_templates.title: Review Staged Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mixpanel-analytics review mode on my staged analytics changes. Focus on PII protection, registry completeness, service patterns, and test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Cron Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mixpanel-analytics skill to design a cron-processed event. Verify whether is_cron_job is needed and include timestamp handling rules.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mixpanel-analytics review mode to audit the full optimo_analytics module. Report P0 through P3 findings with file references and fixes.

output_examples.input: Review staged MixPanel changes for privacy and schema issues.
output: A structured review summary with pass or fail status by category.
A prioritized issue list for PII, registry, schema, service, and test concerns.
Concrete remediation steps for each finding.

output_examples.input: Implement a new event for a completed profile setup action.
output: A proposed event name following the service object action pattern.
A schema and helper plan that avoids names, emails, phone numbers, and raw organization distinct IDs.
A focused test checklist for schema creation, registration, tracking, and non-blocking behavior.

output_examples.input: Check whether a background job event needs cron metadata.
output: A decision on whether is_cron_job applies.
Required timestamp fields when cron metadata is used.
Warnings about event names that should not include cron as a naming component.

best_practices: Run review mode before merging analytics changes that add or modify event properties.
Keep event properties limited to UUID strings, approved organization names, enums, timestamps, and non-PII context.
Run the recommended lint, type, Django, and pytest commands after implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not send names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, or free-text location fields to MixPanel.
Do not use raw organization IDs as distinct_id values without the required prefix.
Do not let MixPanel tracking failures interrupt primary business logic.

faq.question: What repositories is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for the Django4Lyfe backend and its optimo_analytics module. Other projects may need adaptation.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to MixPanel?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation and review instructions for code that sends events. It does not call MixPanel itself.

faq.question: Why does the audit mark medium risk?
faq.answer: The skill asks agents to run shell commands and edit repository files. That is expected for coding work but requires trusted use.

faq.question: Can it detect all PII issues automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides strict checks and review prompts, but human review is still needed for business context.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill text includes compatibility notes for Claude Code and OpenAI Codex workflows.

faq.question: What output should review mode produce?
faq.answer: Review mode should produce a severity-tagged report with category status, file references, issues, fixes, and recommendations.
